preamble: The shift from the lateral affricate [tɬ] to the lateral liquid [l] involves the loss of the stop component, simplifying the articulation. This process, often called lateralization or lenition, reduces the complex affricate to a simple resonant.

examples.note: Historical development or dialectal variation where the affricate simplifies to a lateral liquid.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
